meta.name: em_mdm_FY2026
meta.source: NAMAS lay-description of the 2023 AMA Inpatient MDM chart
meta.effective: AMA CY 2023 (Jan 1) — inpatient place of service
meta.scoring_rule: Final level = highest level met by at least 2 of 3 elements (Problems, Data, Risk); OR documented total time >= the code's threshold.
notes: Risk is an examples-only column per AMA; matches are advisory and must be flagged low_confidence.
Final code needs 2 of 3 elements at the level, OR total documented time at/above the threshold.
This file is loaded alongside foundation_knowledge_FY2026.json at analyze time but is edited and versioned separately.

initial.label: Initial hospital or observation care (new admit)

obs_short.label: Hospital/observation admit & discharge, same calendar date period

problems.label: Number and Complexity of Problems Addressed

criteria: 2 or more self-limited or minor problems
1 acute uncomplicated illness or injury requiring inpatient or observation admit
1 chronic illness with exacerbation, progression, or side effects of treatment
1 undiagnosed new problem with uncertain prognosis
1 or more chronic illnesses with severe exacerbation, progression, or side effects of treatment
1 acute or chronic illness or injury that poses a threat to life or bodily function

data.label: Amount and/or Complexity of Data to be Reviewed and Analyzed

data_item_pool.review_external_records: Review external note(s)/records from an external source
data_item_pool.review_unique_test_result: Review the result(s) of each unique test
data_item_pool.order_unique_test: Order each unique test (imaging, lab, psychometric, physiologic)
data_item_pool.independent_interpretation: Independent interpretation of a test read & billed by another provider (no separate report)
data_item_pool.external_discussion: Discussion of management or test interpretation with an external physician/QHP/source (not separately reported)

low.requirement: Limited — meet at least 1 of 2 categories

options.label: Category 1: any 2 of {external records, unique test result reviewed, unique test ordered}
Category 2: encounter including an additional historian

moderate.requirement: Moderate — meet at least 1 of 3 categories

options.label: Category 1: any combination of 3 from {external records, unique test result, unique test ordered, additional historian}
Category 2: independent interpretation of a test
Category 3: discussion of management/test interpretation with external source

high.requirement: Extensive — meet at least 2 of 3 categories

options.label: Category 1: any combination of 3 from {external records, unique test result, unique test ordered, additional historian}
Category 2: independent interpretation of a test
Category 3: discussion of management/test interpretation with external source

risk.label: Risk of Complications and/or Morbidity or Mortality of Patient Management
risk.note: AMA examples-only column. Matches are advisory; flag low_confidence and never auto-assign a cardiac/HF/PE/DVT diagnosis from a risk match.

examples: Will follow up on patient tomorrow
Medications NOT requiring prescriptive authority (e.g., OTC)
Awaiting discharge without further treatment plan
Initiation, continuation, discontinuation, or modification of a medication requiring prescriptive authority
Decision regarding a minor procedure with documented patient or procedure risk factors
Decision regarding a major procedure without documented patient or procedure risk factors
Patient social or economic conditions impacting care
Consult/referral required for an average/moderate-risk management option
Leaving AMA without high-complexity risk documented
Long/short-term intensive drug monitoring to prevent toxicity (not efficacy monitoring)
Decision regarding a major procedure with documented patient or procedure risk factors
Decision regarding emergency major surgery / major surgery with minimal delay
Decision regarding hospitalization or escalation to a higher level of care
Documentation/discussion of DNR or de-escalation due to poor prognosis
Administration of a controlled substance via IM, IV, or SubQ

scoring.method: Score each of the 3 elements to a level. Visit level = the highest level reached by >= 2 of the 3 elements. Independently, if total documented encounter time >= the code's time threshold, that code is supported by time.
guardrails: Troponin 0-20 pg/mL is normal; never escalate Problems or Risk from troponin alone.
Anticoagulant prophylaxis (Arixtra / Lovenox SubQ / heparin SubQ) does not establish DVT, PE, or atrial fibrillation.
Every Problems or Risk match must cite explicit supporting evidence in the note text.
Risk-tier matches are advisory (low_confidence): they may surface a documentation opportunity but must never auto-assign a diagnosis.
